title: Telangana braces for intense heatwave as temperatures near 47°C
search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds") Telangana
full_text:
HYDERABAD: India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave alert for the next four days, warning of a further rise in temperatures across Telangana.
According to the forecast, 14 districts will be under a red alert on Thursday, while the remaining districts will be under an orange alert. Temperatures are expected to rise gradually by one to two degrees Celsius, with some areas likely to record highs of up to 47 degrees Celsius.
Hyderabad is also expected to witness soaring temperatures, with the mercury likely to cross the 43-degrees Celsius mark.
The severity of the prevailing heat conditions was evident on Wednesday, with as many as 84 locations across Telangana reported heatwave conditions. Two places — one each in Jangaon and Kumuram Bheem Asifabad districts — recorded severe heatwave conditions.
According to data from the Hyderabad Meteorological Centre, the highest maximum temperature in the state was recorded at Dilawarpur in Nirmal district at 46.5 degrees Celsius. Several other districts also witnessed extreme temperatures above 46 degrees Celsius. Hanamkonda and Karimnagar recorded 46.4 degrees Celsius each, while Peddapalli, Jagtial, Jangaon, Khammam, Kumuram Bheem Asifabad, Mancherial and Nizamabad registered temperatures between 46.2 degrees Celsius and 46.3 degrees Celsius.
The IMD stated that light to moderate rainfall is likely at isolated places in some districts today and tomorrow, accompanied by thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds. However, dry weather conditions are expected to prevail from the following day onwards.

title: Telangana reels under heat wave conditions - News Arena India

The CM appealed to people not to venture out of their homes and avoid travelling during the afternoon.
Telangana continues to reel under extreme heat, with as many as 19 districts out of 33 recording maximum temperatures above 46 degrees Celsius and 82 places reporting heatwave conditions.
According to official data released by the Telangana State Development Planning Society, the highest temperature recorded till 5 pm on Wednesday was 46.5 degrees Celsius at Dilawarpur in Nirmal district. It was followed by 46.4 degrees Celsius at Dharmasagar in Hanamkonda district, Choppadandi in Karimnagar district and Odela in Peddapalli district.
Similarly, Raikal in Jagitial district, Jangaon town in Jangaon district, Penuballi in Khammam district, Asifabad in Kumuram Bheem district, Kotapalli in Mancherial district and Balkonda in Nizamabad district recorded 46.3 degrees Celsius.
Several other parts of north Telangana recorded maximum temperatures ranging between 45.8 degrees Celsius and 46 degrees Celsius, the bulletin said, adding that the rest of the state registered temperatures between 41 degrees Celsius and 45 degrees Celsius. Hyderabad, too, recorded maximum temperatures ranging from 41 degrees Celsius to 42.4 degrees Celsius.
The bulletin further said 82 places across 17 districts reported heatwave conditions, with Jangaon and Kumuram Bheem districts witnessing extreme heatwave conditions.
Meanwhile, Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy reviewed the prevailing heatwave situation in the state at an official meeting following a warning issued by the India Meteorological Department (IMD). He appealed to people to remain vigilant and take precautionary measures.
According to an official statement, the Chief Minister directed officials to take all necessary precautionary measures and ensure the availability of adequate ORS packets, supply of drinking water and uninterrupted power supply to residents.
He also directed the medical and health department to provide better treatment to patients suffering from sunstroke and admitted to hospitals. Local authorities were instructed to set up free drinking water supply centres in urban areas.
Appealing to people not to venture out of their homes and avoid travelling during the afternoon, Reddy ordered chief secretary K Ramakrishna Rao to continuously monitor the situation in the districts and issue necessary guidelines to district collectors to ensure public safety during the summer.

title: Telangana Weather Today: 82 places report heatwave as Mercury crosses 46 Degrees - News9live
full_text:
Telangana Weather Today: 82 places report heatwave as Mercury crosses 46 Degrees
Telangana is reeling under a sharp heatwave, with official data showing 19 districts recording temperatures above 46 degrees Celsius. The state government has asked people to avoid afternoon travel and directed officials to maintain drinking water supply, ORS packets and care for sunstroke patients.
New Delhi: Telangana is facing a severe heatwave spell, with 19 districts crossing 46 degrees Celsius on Wednesday, according to official data released by the Telangana State Development Planning Society. The highest temperature stood at 46.5 degrees Celsius at Dilawarpur in Nirmal district till 5 pm.
The heat has now become a public health concern, not just a weather update. With 82 places across 17 districts reporting heatwave conditions, officials have been asked to keep ORS packets ready, maintain drinking water supply and support hospitals treating sunstroke cases.
Telangana heatwave puts health system on alert
North Telangana saw some of the harshest temperatures. Dharmasagar in Hanamkonda, Choppadandi in Karimnagar and Odela in Peddapalli recorded 46.4 degrees Celsius. Raikal, Jangaon town, Penuballi, Asifabad, Kotapalli and Balkonda touched 46.3 degrees Celsius.
Hyderabad was hot too, with temperatures ranging between 41 degrees Celsius and 42.4 degrees Celsius. For many residents, this is the kind of afternoon where even a short walk to buy milk feels like a bad idea.
CM Revanth Reddy urges caution
Telangana Chief Minister A Revanth Reddy reviewed the heatwave situation after an IMD warning. He appealed to people to stay alert and take basic safety steps.
Officials have been directed to keep drinking water centres running in urban areas. Hospitals have been told to give better care to sunstroke patients. The state has also been asked to maintain uninterrupted power supply, a big concern during peak summer hours.
What people should do during the heatwave
People have been advised not to step out during the afternoon. Those who must travel should carry water, cover their head and avoid long exposure to direct sun.
Children, elderly people, outdoor workers and those already unwell face higher risk. Symptoms like dizziness, heavy sweating, headache, weakness or confusion should not be ignored.
Telangana’s weather map looks dangerously hot. The state government has asked chief secretary K Ramakrishna Rao to monitor districts and guide collectors so that public safety measures move fast.

title: Hyderabad Weather Today, May 21: Heatwave Grips City As Temperature Nears 40°C - NativePlanet
full_text:
Hyderabad continues to witness intense summer conditions on Thursday, May 21, 2026, with heatwave conditions persisting across several districts of Telangana. While scattered cloud cover and isolated rain possibilities are expected in parts of the state, the capital city is likely to remain largely hot and dry through most of the day, with temperatures touching around 40°C and "feels like" conditions climbing even higher.
The latest weather pattern indicates that residents may experience a combination of scorching daytime temperatures and uncomfortable humidity levels, making outdoor movement difficult during peak afternoon hours.
Hyderabad Temperature Today
The city began the day at around 29°C, with partly cloudy conditions during the early morning hours. However, temperatures are expected to rise steadily as the day progresses.
Expected weather pattern for Hyderabad on May 21:
- Rain probability: Low, with isolated chances during afternoon hours
Despite some cloud movement later in the day, significant relief from heat is unlikely.
Heatwave Alert Continues Across Telangana
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has continued warnings for multiple districts in Telangana as temperatures remain unusually high. Reports indicate that several locations across the state have crossed the 46°C mark, highlighting the intensity of the ongoing heatwave.
Among the hottest locations recorded:
- Multiple districts including Peddapalli, Jagtial, Khammam and Kumuram Bheem Asifabad crossed 46°C
Heatwave conditions have reportedly affected dozens of locations across Telangana, with some areas moving into severe categories.
Will Hyderabad Receive Rain Today?
Rainfall chances remain uncertain for Hyderabad. Forecast models suggest isolated cloud development during afternoon hours, with only a limited possibility of light showers or thunderstorms.
While some regions of Telangana may witness evening rainfall accompanied by gusty winds and lightning activity, Hyderabad's overall rainfall probability remains relatively low. Any precipitation, if it occurs, is expected to be brief and localised rather than widespread.
Weather Advisory For Residents
Authorities and weather experts continue to advise precautionary measures as temperatures remain elevated:
- Monitor weather updates for sudden thunderstorm developments
Although occasional cloud cover may temporarily reduce direct sunshine, Hyderabad is expected to remain under the influence of strong summer conditions through the day, with meaningful relief likely dependent on broader pre-monsoon developments in the coming days.
